OPPOSITION TO PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT TO COMPEL DEPOSITION (TRANSACTION ID # 15069064) FILED BY DEFENDANT ABDULLAH, YUSEF MOHAMED

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT YUSEF ABDULLAH'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT YUSEF MOHAMED ABDULLAH MET HIS INITIAL BURDEN PRESENTING EVIDENCE THAT SHOWS THAT PLAINTIFF CANNOT ESTABLISH THAT DEFENDANT ACTED NEGLIGENTLY OR THAT ANY ALLEGED NEGLIGENCE BY DEFENDANT WAS A SUBSTANTIAL FACTOR IN CAUSING PLAINTIFF'S HARM. (DEFENDANT'S UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS ("UMF") 1-5.) PLAINTIFF DOES NOT SUBMIT ANY EVIDENCE IN OPPOSITION. GIVEN THAT IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT DEFENDANT WAS ABLE TO STOP HIS VEHICLE WITHOUT HITTING PLAINTIFF'S VEHICLE (UMF 4), THERE IS NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO WHETHER DEFENDANT WAS FOLLOWING PLAINTIFF'S VEHICLE TOO CLOSELY. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312 AND CCP 437C(G). JUDGE ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER DENISE DOUCETTE, CSR NO. 5963 (302/EHG)
MINI MINUTES FOR APR-30-2015 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-24-2015 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-30-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT YUSEF ABDULLAH'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant Yusef Mohamed Abdullah met his initial burden presenting evidence that shows that Plaintiff cannot establish that Defendant acted negligently or that any alleged negligence by Defendant was a substantial factor in causing Plaintiff's harm. (Defendant's Undisputed Material Facts ("UMF") 1-5.) Plaintiff does not submit any evidence in opposition. Given that it is undisputed that Defendant was able to stop his vehicle without hitting Plaintiff's vehicle (UMF 4), there is no triable issue of material fact as to whether Defendant was following Plaintiff's vehicle too closely. Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312 and CCP 437C(g). Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Denise Doucette, CSR No. 5963; Dept. 302.

Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Apr-02-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. MICHAEL S. MORSE of Law Office of Catherine A. Walsh, Tel No. (925) 825-9500 appearing for Defendant Yusef Mohamed Abdullah;
Atty. KELLI E. GEORGE of Law Offices of Melissa M. Ballard - Hartford Insurance, Tel No. (714) 571-0407 appearing telephonically for Defendant Janice Furman;
No appearance made by or for Plaintiff Nicole Goddard.

RULING - Matter was not argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF NICOLE GODDARD'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint is denied. Mr. Abdullah does not rely on a pleading defect in his motion for summary judgment and appears to indicate that the complaint is well-pleaded as to him. See CCP 452. Accordingly, the amendment is unnecessary. In addition, allowing amendment at this juncture will prejudice Mr. Abdullah. An amended complaint will render his pending motion for summary judgment moot. See State Compensation Ins. Fund v. Sup. Crt. 184 Cal.App.4th 1124, 1130-1131. Mr. Abdullah will be required to file a new motion for summary judgment with at least 75-days notice, pay a new filing fee, and it is unclear whether the court would allow its calendaring based on the current trial date. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
